zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord John or Jean Le Belward於1050年出生在英國爾馬爾帕斯時，他的父親Lord John William Belward37歲，母親Lady John William Belward37歲。他和Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley至少有1個兒子。他去世於1134年在英國享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord John or Jean Le Belward于1050年出生在英国爾馬爾帕斯时，他的父亲Lord John William Belward37岁，母亲Lady John William Belward37岁。他和Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley至少有1个儿子。他去世于1134年在英国享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Belward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Staffordshire and Welsh Marches): from the patronymic ap Edward ‘son of Edward’ (see Edward ).

variants.name: Beddard
meanings: Welsh (Staffordshire): variant of   Bedward  .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name Edward (Old English Ēadweard, from ēad ‘prosperity, fortune’ + w(e)ard ‘guard, ward’). The popularity of the personal name was largely due to its being the name of the sainted Anglo-Saxon king Edward the Confessor and of three kings in the 13th–14th centuries.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord John or Jean Le Belward was born in 1050, in Malpas, Cheshire, England, his father, Lord John William Belward, was 37 and his mother, Lady John William Belward, was 37. He had at least 1 son with Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. He died in 1134, in England, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord John or Jean Le Belward est né en 1050, à Malpas, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord John William Belward, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Lady John William Belward, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. Il est décédé en 1134, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord John or Jean Le Belward 1050 in Malpas, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord John William Belward 37 und seine Mutter Lady John William Belward 37. Er hatte mit Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1134 im Alter von 84 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John or Jean Le Belward è nato il nel 1050 nel Malpas, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Lord John William Belward, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Lady John William Belward, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. Morì nel 1134, nel England, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.Malpas, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアマルプアス
ja.lifeSummary: Lord John or Jean Le Belwardが1050年にイギリスチェシュアイアマルプアスで生まれた時，父Lord John William Belwardは37歳，母Lady John William Belwardは37歳であった。彼はLady Ellen De Cholmondeleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1134年にイギリスで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord John or Jean Le Belward(이)가 1050년 영국 체실르 맬파스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord John William Belward(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady John William Belward(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1134년 영국에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John or Jean Le Belward nasceu em 1050, em Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord John William Belward, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Lady John William Belward, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. Ele faleceu em 1134, em Inglaterra, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord John or Jean Le Belward родился в 1050 г. в местности малпас, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord John William Belward было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady John William Belward, 37. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. Он умер в 1134 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord John or Jean Le Belward nació en 1050, en Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord John William Belward, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Lady John William Belward, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady Ellen De Cholmondeley. Murió en 1134, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años.
